markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define optional SCIP/LSP-style import format as a Handshake-owned projection/import path, not an external service dependency. This MT belongs to CodeIndexingAndNavigation. Index code structure and expose precise backend navigation for agents.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Code navigation must use Handshake-managed parsers/adapters and store spans/citations in PostgreSQL.
Tree-sitter is the first parser family; LSP/SCIP-style precision is a Handshake-owned import/projection path, not an outside service requirement.
scope.expected_behavior: ScipLspImportBoundary leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ScipLspImportBoundary is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 commits 5d6490b0 (compiling foundation) + 79e1a7e4 (runtime proof); 43 lib + 11 integration + 1 nav-API tests green on real PostgreSQL under runtime-full,test-utils. Found+fixed impl-Type/struct-Type entity collision. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: Path traversal NOT fully blocked: validate (scip.rs:170) rejects backslash and leading '/', but does NOT reject '../' sequences or '.'/'..' segments. A document relative_path '../../etc/passwd' or 'a/../../b' PASSES validation despite the docstring claiming 'repo-relative POSIX'. Today the engine uses relative_path only as an entity_key/display_name string (no filesystem write is wired in the reviewed code), so it is not yet an arbitrary-write primitive - but the moment any consumer joins this path to an FS anchor (cf. read_and_index at engine.rs:1100 which does fs_anchor.join(relative_path)), a malicious artifact escapes the repo root. Defense-in-depth: reject '..' segments at import validation.

Unbounded memory on a hostile artifact: parse_scip_artifact (scip.rs:149) calls serde_json::from_slice on the WHOLE byte buffer with no size cap and no documents/symbols/occurrences count cap. A multi-GB or billions-of-symbols artifact OOMs the process (DoS). The import is operator-gated, but 'operator-provided' is not 'trusted-to-be-small'.

Documented invariant not enforced: the comment at scip.rs:193 says 'Every occurrence must reference a symbol declared SOMEWHERE in the artifact', but validate() only checks byte ranges (scip.rs:196-202) - it never verifies occurrence.symbol is in the declared symbol set. An occurrence referencing an undeclared symbol passes; when projected to edges it would dangle or create a phantom target. Dead comment / missing check.
evasion_probes: Confirmed PARSE-ONLY: scip.rs imports only serde + sha2; parse_scip_artifact does serde_json::from_slice then validate; no process spawn, no std::process, no IO, no LSP. The MT's core claim (never spawn an indexer/LSP) holds.

Confirmed strict shape rejection: non-JSON, empty documents, empty symbol id/display_name, inverted byte ranges all -> typed ScipImportRejected (delegate tests rejects_non_json / rejects_empty_documents / rejects_absolute_path / rejects_inverted_byte_range).

Confirmed artifact_hash is sha256 over bytes (fidelity hash for the import ledger 0171) and record_knowledge_scip_import requires a reason for non-imported status (knowledge.rs:5116) - rejected imports are auditable.
byte_start/byte_end are u64; no overflow path. line_start/end are u32; a degenerate line_end<line_start is NOT validated (only byte ranges are) - minor, lines are advisory.

hardening_closed.note: Adversarial finding fixed + real-PG/unit test green (knowledge_code_index_tests 13 + nav_api 2 + 59 unit, 0 fail).

evidence: Hardened 575fcffd: SCIP validate rejects ../ and . segments; 64MiB byte cap + doc/symbol/occurrence count caps; occurrence->declared-symbol invariant enforced.

adversarial_review_v2.note: VERIFIED FIXED from V1 (575fcffd): parse_scip_artifact enforces a 64 MiB byte cap FIRST (scip.rs:152,162) + document/symbol/occurrence count caps (153-155), and validate_relative_path rejects leading '/', backslash, and '.'/'..'/empty segments (scip.rs:187) with tests rejects_dot_dot_path_segment (362) + rejects_single_dot_path_segment (374). The path-traversal vector the V1 reviewer flagged (../ passing validation) is closed in real code, not just the docstring.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-105 requires defining an optional SCIP/LSP-style index format as a Handshake-OWNED import/projection boundary (never an external LSP/indexer service), with durable PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, strict typed rejection, and validator-readable proof. The code delivers exactly this: a parse+validate-only scip.rs boundary, a real PG import-ledger migration (0171), a real sqlx insert path, and a real-PostgreSQL integration test recording imported and rejected outcomes.
findings: scip.rs:161-177 parse_scip_artifact is PARSE+VALIDATE ONLY: imports only serde+sha2, does serde_json::from_slice then validate, no std::process / no IO / no LSP spawn. Satisfies the MT core claim 'never spawns an indexer/LSP' and current spec 2.3.13.11 ('LSP/SCIP-style precision is a Handshake-owned import/projection path, NOT an outside service requirement', 02-system-architecture.md:2694).

Durable PostgreSQL authority is real, not mock: migrations/0171_knowledge_code_scip_imports.sql creates knowledge_code_scip_imports with artifact_format/status CHECK constraints, KSCIP-id + sha256 regex CHECKs, FK to workspaces and kernel_event_ledger, and a reason-shape constraint (status='imported' OR reason IS NOT NULL) so rejected/partial imports are auditable and never silent.

storage/knowledge.rs:6622 record_knowledge_scip_import performs a real sqlx INSERT into self.pool() with server-side validation (sha256 hash gate 6626, required reason for non-imported 6631-6637). No SQLite / no in-memory substitute.

Real-PostgreSQL proof exists: tests/knowledge_code_index_tests.rs:1668 mt105_scip_import_ledger_records_outcomes gates on knowledge_pg(), parses a valid artifact, records an 'imported' row and a 'rejected' row (with the parse rejection reason), then asserts list_knowledge_scip_imports returns 2. This touches the Handshake-owned PG ledger contract, not implementer mocks (sub_rule_2 PASS).

V1 adversarial security findings are actually closed in committed code (commit 575fcffd): path-traversal '..'/'.'/empty-segment rejection via is_repo_relative_posix (scip.rs:182-188), 64MiB byte cap checked before deserialisation (scip.rs:163-170) plus document/symbol/occurrence count caps (scip.rs:198-222), and the occurrence->declared-symbol invariant now enforced (scip.rs:274-282) with covering unit tests rejects_dot_dot_path_segment / rejects_artifact_exceeding_byte_cap / rejects_occurrence_referencing_undeclared_symbol / accepts cross-document reference.
sub_rule_3 PASS: lifecycle.claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER-20260611-071323, completed_by=null; no self-certified COMPLETED transition was signed by the implementer.

Non-blocking aside: engine.rs does not yet call parse_scip_artifact / record_knowledge_scip_import (the import path is not wired into the indexing orchestrator), and the V1 fs_anchor.join sink at engine.rs:1100 is the Tree-sitter file-index path, not the SCIP import path. The MT scope is to define the owned boundary + durable ledger, which is delivered and proven; engine projection wiring is a downstream concern, not an MT-105 acceptance gap.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
